CCSS.Math.Content.4.MD.A.1||Know relativ: Your child knows how big different measurement units are within one system (like kilometers, meters, centimeters) and can change measurements from bigger units to smaller units and record these conversions in a table.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.MD.A||Solve problems: Your child can solve problems that involve measuring and changing measurements from bigger units to smaller units.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.NBT.B.6||Find whole-: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by one-digit numbers using place value and multiplication/division facts, and explain their work with equations or pictures.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.NBT.B.5||Multiply a: Your child can multiply a number up to four digits by a one-digit number and multiply two two-digit numbers using place value and properties of multiplication, and explain their work with equations or pictures.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.NBT.B.4||Fluently ad: Your child can quickly add and subtract large whole numbers using the standard method.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.NBT.B||Use place val: Your child uses place value and math rules to add, subtract, multiply, and divide multi-digit numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.NBT.A.3||Use place v: Your child can round multi-digit whole numbers to any place value.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.NBT.A.2||Read and wr: Your child can read and write large whole numbers in digits, words, and expanded form, and compare two numbers using >, =, or < based on their digits.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.NBT.A.1||Recognize t: Your child understands that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place is worth ten times what it is in the place to its right.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.NBT.A||Generalize pl: Your child understands place value for large whole numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.OA.C.5||Generate a n: Your child can create number or shape patterns that follow a rule and notice features in the pattern that the rule doesn't directly say.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.OA.C||Generate and a: Your child can make and study patterns.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.OA.B.4||Find all fac: Your child can find all factor pairs of numbers from 1 to 100, know that numbers are multiples of their factors, decide if a number is a multiple of a one-digit number, and tell if a number is prime or composite.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.OA.B||Gain familiari: Your child understands factors and multiples.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.OA.A.3||Solve multis: Your child can solve word problems with several steps using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division, including problems with remainders, write equations with letters for unknowns, and check if answers make sense by estimating.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.OA.A.2||Multiply or: Your child can solve word problems comparing amounts by multiplying or dividing, using drawings and equations with symbols for unknown numbers, and tell the difference between multiplying and adding comparisons.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.OA.A.1||Interpret a: Your child understands multiplication equations as comparisons, like seeing 35 = 5 × 7 as 35 is 5 times 7, and can write multiplication equations from words about comparisons.
CCSS.Math.Content.4.OA.A||Use the four o: Your child can use addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with whole numbers to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.C.9||(+) Prove: Your child can prove formulas for adding and subtracting sine, cosine, and tangent and use these formulas to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.C.8||Prove the: Your child can prove the Pythagorean identity sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1 and use it to find sine, cosine, or tangent values when given one value and the angle's quadrant.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.C||Prove and ap: Your child can prove and use trigonometric identities.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.B.7||(+) Use in: Your child can use inverse trig functions to solve equations from real-world problems, check answers with technology, and explain what the answers mean.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.B.6||(+) Unders: Your child understands that limiting a trig function to a part where it only goes up or only goes down lets you find its inverse.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.B.5||Choose tri: Your child can pick trig functions to model repeating patterns with specific height, speed, and middle value.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.B||Model period: Your child can use trig functions to model things that repeat over time.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.A.4||(+) Use th: Your child can use the unit circle to explain how trig functions repeat and show symmetry.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.A.3||(+) Use sp: Your child can use special triangles to find sine, cosine, and tangent values for certain angles and use the unit circle to find values for related angles.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.A.2||Explain ho: Your child understands how the unit circle helps extend trig functions to all real numbers by measuring angles in radians.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.A.1||Understand: Your child understands that radian measure of an angle is the length of the arc it makes on the unit circle.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-TF.A||Extend the d: Your child can use the unit circle to extend trig functions to all angles.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-LE.B.5||Interpret: Your child can explain what the parts of a linear or exponential function mean in a real situation.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-LE.B||Interpret ex: Your child can explain what function expressions mean based on the situation they describe.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-LE.A.4||For expone: Your child can write the solution to an exponential equation as a logarithm and use technology to find its value.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-LE.A.3||Observe us: Your child can see from graphs and tables that things growing exponentially will eventually be bigger than things growing linearly, quadratically, or by other polynomial rules.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-LE.A.2||Construct: Your child can create linear and exponential functions, including sequences, from graphs, descriptions, or pairs of input and output values.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-LE.A.1c||Recognize: Your child can recognize when something grows or shrinks by the same percent each time.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-LE.A.1b||Recognize: Your child can recognize when something changes by the same amount each time.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-LE.A.1a||Prove tha: Your child understands that linear functions grow by equal amounts over equal steps, and exponential functions grow by equal factors over equal steps.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-LE.A.1||Distinguis: Your child can tell the difference between situations modeled by linear functions and those modeled by exponential functions.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-LE.A||Construct an: Your child can build and compare linear, quadratic, and exponential models and solve related problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.B.5||(+) Unders: Your child understands that exponents and logarithms undo each other and can use this to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.B.4d||(+) Produ: Your child can make a function that can be reversed by limiting its inputs.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.B.4c||(+) Read: Your child can find values of an inverse function from a graph or table when the inverse exists.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.B.4b||(+) Verif: Your child can check that two functions are inverses by combining them and seeing if they undo each other.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.B.4a||Solve an: Your child can solve simple equations with functions that have inverses and write the inverse function.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.B.3||Identify t: Your child can describe how changing a function by adding, multiplying, or shifting affects its graph and can find these changes from graphs; they can also recognize even and odd functions from graphs and formulas.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.B||Build new fu: Your child can create new functions by combining or changing existing ones.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.A.2||Write arit: Your child can write arithmetic and geometric sequences using formulas or steps, use them to model situations, and switch between the two forms.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.A.1b||Combine s: Your child can combine common types of functions by adding, subtracting, multiplying, or dividing them.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.A.1a||Determine: Your child can find a formula, a step-by-step process, or calculation method from a real-life situation.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.A.1||Write a fu: Your child can write a rule that shows how two amounts are related.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-BF.A||Build a func: Your child will create a function that represents how two quantities are connected.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.C.9||Compare pr: Your child can compare two functions shown in different ways, like with equations, graphs, tables, or words.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.C.8b||Use the p: Your child understands how to use exponent rules to explain parts of exponential functions.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.C.8a||Use the p: Your child can factor and complete the square in quadratic functions to find zeros, highest or lowest points, and symmetry, and explain what these mean in real situations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.C.8||Write a fu: Your child can rewrite a function in different but equal ways to show and explain different features of the function.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.C.7e||Graph exp: Your child can draw graphs of exponential, logarithmic, and trigonometric functions, showing important points like intercepts, repeating patterns, and height.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.C.7d||(+) Graph: Your child can graph rational functions, find zeros and lines the graph approaches, and show how the graph behaves at the ends.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.C.7c||Graph pol: Your child can graph polynomial functions, find zeros when possible, and show how the graph behaves at the ends.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.C.7b||Graph squ: Your child can graph square root, cube root, step, and absolute value functions.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.C.7a||Graph lin: Your child can graph linear and quadratic functions and show where they cross the axes and their highest or lowest points.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.C.7||Graph func: Your child can draw graphs of functions by hand for simple cases and use technology for more complex ones, showing important features.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.C||Analyze func: Your child understands how to study functions using different ways to show them.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.B.6||Calculate: Your child can find and explain the average rate of change of a function using formulas, tables, or graphs.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.B.5||Relate the: Your child understands how the inputs (domain) of a function relate to its graph and the quantities it describes.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.B.4||For a func: Your child can explain important parts of graphs and tables for functions that show relationships, and draw graphs from descriptions.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.B||Interpret fu: Your child understands what functions mean in real-life situations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.A.3||Recognize: Your child understands that sequences are special functions with inputs that are whole numbers, sometimes defined by rules using earlier terms.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.A.2||Use functi: Your child can use function notation, find outputs for inputs, and explain what function statements mean in real situations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.A.1||Understand: Your child understands that a function pairs each input with exactly one output, and that the graph shows these pairs as points.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSF-IF.A||Understand t: Your child understands what a function is and how to use function notation.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NF.A.3d||Compare two: Your child can compare two fractions with the same top or bottom number by thinking about their size, and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NF.A.3c||Express who: Your child can write whole numbers as fractions and recognize when fractions are equal to whole numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NF.A.3b||Recognize a: Your child can find and explain simple fractions that are equal, like 1/2 and 2/4, using pictures or models.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NF.A.3a||Understand: Your child understands that two fractions are equal if they represent the same amount or point on a number line.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NF.A.3||Explain equi: Your child can explain when fractions are equal and compare their sizes by thinking about what they mean.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NF.A.2b||Represent a: Your child can show a fraction a/b on a number line by marking off a parts of size 1/b starting from zero.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NF.A.2a||Represent a: Your child can show the fraction 1/b on a number line by dividing the space from 0 to 1 into b equal parts.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NF.A.2||Understand a: Your child understands fractions as numbers on a number line and can show them there.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NF.A.1||Understand a: Your child understands that 1/b means one part when a whole is split into b equal parts, and a/b means a parts of size 1/b.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NF.A||Develop unders: Your child is learning to understand fractions as numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.G.A.2||Partition sha: Your child can divide shapes into equal parts with the same area and say the area of each part as a fraction of the whole.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.G.A.1||Understand th: Your child understands that shapes like rhombuses, rectangles, and squares share some features, and can name bigger groups they belong to, like quadrilaterals.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.G.A||Reason with sha: Your child can think about shapes and their features.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.D.8||Solve real w: Your child can solve problems about the perimeter of shapes, including finding missing side lengths and comparing shapes with the same perimeter or area.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.D||Geometric meas: Your child understands that perimeter measures the distance around a shape and can tell the difference between length and area.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.C.7d||Recognize a: Your child can find the area of shapes made of rectangles by breaking them apart and adding the areas, and use this to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.C.7c||Use tiling: Your child can use tiles or area models to show that the area of a rectangle with side lengths a and b + c is the sum of the areas of rectangles with sides a and b, and a and c.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.C.7b||Multiply si: Your child can multiply whole number side lengths to find the area of rectangles and use this to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.C.7a||Find the ar: Your child can find the area of a rectangle by covering it with tiles and understand that this is the same as multiplying the side lengths.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.C.7||Relate area: Your child can connect finding area with multiplying and adding numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.C.6||Measure area: Your child can find the area of shapes by counting squares of different sizes like square centimeters or square inches.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.C.5b||A plane fig: Your child understands that if a flat shape can be covered exactly by a certain number of squares, its area is that many square units.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.C.5a||A square wi: Your child knows a square with sides one unit long is called a unit square and has an area of one square unit to measure area.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.C.5||Recognize ar: Your child understands that area is a way to describe flat shapes and learns how to measure it.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.C||Geometric meas: Your child learns about area and how it relates to multiplying and adding.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.B.4||Generate mea: Your child can measure lengths using rulers with halves and quarters of an inch and show the results on a line plot.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.B.3||Draw a scale: Your child can draw picture and bar graphs with correct scales to show data and solve simple problems about how many more or less.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.A.2||Measure and: Your child can measure and estimate liquid amounts and weights using grams, kilograms, and liters, and solve simple math problems about them.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.A.1||Tell and wri: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest minute, measure time intervals, and solve problems adding or subtracting time.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.MD.A||Solve problems: Your child can solve problems about measuring and estimating time, liquids, and weights.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NBT.A.3||Multiply on: Your child can multiply one-digit numbers by multiples of 10 like 20 or 90 using place value ideas.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NBT.A.2||Fluently ad: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 1000 quickly using place value and other strategies.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NBT.A.1||Use place v: Your child can round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100 using place value.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.NBT.A||Use place val: Your child uses place value and math rules to add, subtract, multiply, and divide bigger numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.D.9||Identify ari: Your child can find and explain number patterns in addition and multiplication tables.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.D.8||Solve two-st: Your child can solve two-step word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division and check if answers make sense.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.D||Solve problems: Your child can solve problems using all four operations and recognize patterns in math.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.C.7||Fluently mul: Your child can multiply and divide numbers up to 100 quickly and knows all multiplication facts for one-digit numbers by memory.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.C||Multiply and d: Your child can multiply and divide numbers up to 100.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.B.6||Understand d: Your child understands division as finding a missing number in multiplication.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.B.5||Apply proper: Your child uses math rules to help multiply and divide.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.B||Understand pro: Your child understands multiplication rules and how multiplication and division are related.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.A.4||Determine th: Your child can find the missing number in multiplication or division equations with three numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.A.3||Use multipli: Your child can solve word problems using multiplication and division with equal groups, arrays, or measurements, using drawings or equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.A.2||Interpret wh: Your child understands division as sharing objects equally or grouping them into equal parts.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.A.1||Interpret pr: Your child understands multiplication as finding the total number in several equal groups.
CCSS.Math.Content.3.OA.A||Represent and: Your child can represent and solve problems using multiplication and division.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.SP.B.5d||Relating th: Your child can choose the best way to describe data based on its shape and the situation it came from.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.SP.B.5c||Giving quan: Your child can find and explain numbers that show the center and spread of data and describe patterns or unusual points.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.SP.B.5b||Describing: Your child can describe what is being measured in data, how it was measured, and the units used.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.SP.B.5a||Reporting t: Your child can tell how many data points were collected.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.SP.B.5||Summarize nu: Your child can summarize sets of numbers by explaining what they mean in their real-life context.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.SP.B.4||Display nume: Your child can show number data on number lines using dot plots, histograms, and box plots.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.SP.B||Summarize and: Your child can summarize and describe how data is spread out and shaped.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.SP.A.3||Recognize th: Your child understands that one number can show the center of data and another number can show how much the data varies.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.SP.A.2||Understand t: Your child understands that data collected to answer a question has a pattern that can be described by its center, spread, and shape.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.SP.A.1||Recognize a: Your child knows a statistical question expects different answers because the data can vary.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.SP.A||Develop unders: Your child learns about how data can vary and what that means.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.C.9||Use variable: Your child can use letters to show how two changing amounts are related, write equations for these, and understand the relationship by looking at graphs and tables.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.C||Represent and: Your child can show and study how one number changes when another number changes.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.B.8||Write an ine: Your child can write inequalities like x > c or x < c to show limits in problems and draw their solutions on a number line.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.B.7||Solve real-w: Your child can solve real-life and math problems by writing and solving simple equations like x + p = q or px = q with positive numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.B.6||Use variable: Your child can use letters to stand for numbers and write expressions to solve problems, understanding that letters can mean unknown or any number in a group.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.B.5||Understand s: Your child understands that solving an equation or inequality means finding which numbers make it true and can check this by plugging in numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.B||Reason about a: Your child can think through and solve equations and inequalities that have one variable.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.A.4||Identify whe: Your child can tell when two expressions are equal because they give the same result no matter what number is used.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.A.3||Apply the pr: Your child can use math rules to create expressions that mean the same thing.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.A.2c||Evaluate ex: Your child can find the value of expressions when numbers are plugged in, including using exponents and following the correct order of operations.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.A.2b||Identify pa: Your child can name parts of an expression like sum, product, factor, and coefficient, and see parts of an expression as one piece.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.A.2a||Write expre: Your child can write expressions that show math operations using numbers and letters for numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.A.2||Write, read,: Your child can write, read, and find the value of expressions where letters stand for numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.A.1||Write and ev: Your child can write and calculate expressions that include whole-number exponents.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.EE.A||Apply and exte: Your child can use what they know about arithmetic to work with expressions that include variables.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C.8||Solve real-w: Your child can solve problems by plotting points in all four parts of the coordinate plane and use coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C.7d||Distinguish: Your child can tell the difference between comparing absolute values and comparing which number is bigger or smaller.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C.7c||Understand: Your child understands absolute value as how far a number is from zero on the number line and can explain this in real-life situations.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C.7b||Write, inte: Your child can write and explain order statements about rational numbers in real-world examples.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C.7a||Interpret s: Your child can understand inequalities as showing where numbers are positioned compared to each other on a number line.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C.7||Understand o: Your child understands how to order rational numbers and knows what absolute value means.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C.6c||Find and po: Your child can find and place integers and rational numbers on number lines and on coordinate planes.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C.6b||Understand: Your child understands that the signs in ordered pairs show where points are on the coordinate plane and can see how points reflect across axes.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C.6a||Recognize o: Your child knows that opposite signs mean numbers are on opposite sides of zero and that the opposite of the opposite number is the original number.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C.6||Understand a: Your child understands that rational numbers can be shown as points on number lines and coordinate planes, including negative numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C.5||Understand t: Your child knows that positive and negative numbers describe things going in opposite directions or having opposite values and can explain what zero means in these situations.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.C||Apply and exte: Your child can use what they know about numbers to understand and work with rational numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.B.4||Find the gre: Your child can find the greatest common factor of two numbers up to 100 and the least common multiple of two numbers up to 12, and use these to rewrite sums in simpler forms.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.B.3||Fluently add: Your child can quickly add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals with many digits using standard methods.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.B.2||Fluently div: Your child can quickly divide large numbers using the usual steps.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.B||Compute fluent: Your child can work quickly with large numbers and find common factors and multiples.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.A.1||Interpret an: Your child can understand and calculate division of fractions by fractions and solve related word problems using pictures and equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.NS.A||Apply and exte: Your child can use what they know about multiplication and division to divide fractions by fractions.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.RP.A.3d||Use ratio r: Your child can use ratio thinking to change measurement units and correctly adjust units when multiplying or dividing.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.RP.A.3c||Find a perc: Your child can find a percent of a number by thinking of it as parts per 100 and solve problems where they find the whole from a part and a percent.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.RP.A.3b||Solve unit: Your child can solve problems about unit rates, like figuring out prices per item or speeds.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.RP.A.3a||Make tables: Your child can make tables of equivalent ratios with whole numbers, find missing numbers, plot these on graphs, and use tables to compare ratios.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.RP.A.3||Use ratio an: Your child can use ratio and rate thinking to solve real-life and math problems using tables, diagrams, or equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.RP.A.2||Understand t: Your child understands what a unit rate is and can use words to describe rates in ratio relationships.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.RP.A.1||Understand t: Your child understands what a ratio is and can use words to explain the relationship between two amounts.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.RP.A||Understand rat: Your child understands ratios and can use ratio thinking to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.G.A.4||Represent thr: Your child can draw flat patterns made of rectangles and triangles that fold into 3D shapes, and use these patterns to find the total surface area of the shapes, solving real-life and math problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.G.A.3||Draw polygons: Your child can plot shapes on a grid using points and find the length of sides when points share the same x or y coordinate, applying this to solve real-world and math problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.G.A.2||Find the volu: Your child can find the volume of a box-shaped figure with fractional side lengths by filling it with smaller cubes and by multiplying the side lengths, using formulas to solve real-world and math problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.G.A.1||Find the area: Your child can find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special four-sided shapes, and polygons by breaking them into rectangles or triangles, and use these methods to solve real-world and math problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.6.G.A||Solve real-worl: Your child can solve problems that involve finding area, surface area, and volume in real-life and math situations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.D.12||Graph th: Your child can draw the solution to a linear inequality on a graph as a shaded half-plane and show where solutions to multiple inequalities overlap.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.D.11||Explain: Your child understands that where two graphs cross shows the solutions to equations set equal, and can find these solutions approximately using graphs, tables, or technology for many types of functions.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.D.10||Understa: Your child understands that the graph of an equation with two variables shows all its solutions as points on a curve or line on a coordinate plane.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.D||Represent a: Your child can show and solve equations and inequalities by drawing their graphs.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.C.9||(+) Find: Your child can find the inverse of a matrix when it exists and use it to solve systems of linear equations, using technology for larger matrices.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.C.8||(+) Repre: Your child can write a system of linear equations as one matrix equation using vectors.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.C.7||Solve a s: Your child can solve simple systems made of one linear and one quadratic equation both by algebra and by graphing.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.C.6||Solve sys: Your child can solve pairs of linear equations exactly and approximately, including by using graphs.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.C.5||Prove tha: Your child can show that changing one equation by adding a multiple of the other does not change the solutions of a system of two equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.B.4b||Solve qu: Your child can solve quadratic equations by different methods like taking square roots, factoring, completing the square, or using the quadratic formula, and can write complex solutions when needed.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.B.4a||Use the: Your child can change any quadratic equation into a form that makes it easier to solve by completing the square and can derive the quadratic formula from this form.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.B.4||Solve qua: Your child can solve quadratic equations with one variable.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.B.3||Solve lin: Your child can solve linear equations and inequalities with one variable, including those with letters as coefficients.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.B||Solve equat: Your child can solve equations and inequalities that have one variable.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.A.2||Solve sim: Your child can solve simple equations with fractions or roots and understand why some solutions might not work.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.A.1||Explain e: Your child can explain each step when solving an equation and give reasons why the method works.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-REI.A||Understand: Your child understands solving equations as a logical process and can explain their thinking.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-CED.A.4||Rearrange: Your child can rearrange formulas to solve for a specific variable, using the same thinking as when solving equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-CED.A.3||Represent: Your child can write equations or inequalities to show limits or conditions and understand which solutions make sense in real situations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-CED.A.2||Create eq: Your child can create equations with two or more variables to show relationships and graph them with labels and scales.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-CED.A.1||Create eq: Your child can write and use equations and inequalities with one variable to solve problems, including those from linear, quadratic, rational, and exponential functions.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-CED.A||Create equa: Your child can create equations that describe numbers or relationships between quantities.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-APR.D.7||(+) Under: Your child understands that rational expressions behave like numbers and can add, subtract, multiply, and divide them.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-APR.D.6||Rewrite s: Your child can rewrite simple rational expressions in different ways, including dividing polynomials to write them as a whole part plus a fraction.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-APR.C.5||(+) Know: Your child can use the Binomial Theorem to expand expressions like (x + y)ⁿ and find the coefficients using methods like Pascal's Triangle.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-APR.C.4||Prove pol: Your child can prove polynomial identities and use them to explain number relationships.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-APR.C||Use polynom: Your child can use known polynomial identities to solve math problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-APR.B.3||Identify: Your child can find where a polynomial equals zero by factoring and use those points to sketch a rough graph of the polynomial.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-APR.B.2||Know and: Your child knows that when dividing a polynomial by (x - a), the remainder is the value of the polynomial at a, and that (x - a) is a factor if this value is zero.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-APR.B||Understand: Your child understands how the zeros of polynomials relate to their factors.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-APR.A.1||Understan: Your child understands that polynomials can be added, subtracted, and multiplied like whole numbers, and can perform these operations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-APR.A||Perform ari: Your child can add, subtract, and multiply polynomials.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-SSE.B.4||Derive th: Your child can find and use the formula for the sum of a finite geometric series when the ratio is not 1 to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-SSE.B.3c||Use the: Your child can use the rules of exponents to rewrite expressions for exponential functions.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-SSE.B.3b||Complete: Your child can complete the square in a quadratic expression to find its highest or lowest value.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-SSE.B.3a||Factor a: Your child can break down a quadratic expression to find the values where the function equals zero.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-SSE.B.3||Choose an: Your child can rewrite an expression in different ways to show and explain its important parts.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-SSE.B||Write expre: Your child can write expressions in different but equal forms to help solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-SSE.A.2||Use the s: Your child can look at how an expression is built to find new ways to rewrite it.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-SSE.A.1b||Interpre: Your child can understand complicated expressions by treating parts of them as single pieces.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-SSE.A.1a||Interpre: Your child can explain parts of an expression, like terms, factors, and numbers in front.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-SSE.A.1||Interpret: Your child understands what an expression means based on the situation it describes.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSA-SSE.A||Interpret t: Your child understands how expressions are put together.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-MG.A.3||Apply geom: Your child can use geometry to solve design problems, like creating objects that meet size or cost limits.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-MG.A.2||Apply conc: Your child can use ideas about density with area and volume to understand real-world situations, like people per square mile.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-MG.A.1||Use geomet: Your child can describe objects by using shapes and their measurements, like seeing a tree trunk as a cylinder.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-MG.A||Apply geomet: Your child can use geometry ideas to model and solve real-life problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GMD.B.4||Identify: Your child can identify the shapes you get when slicing 3D objects and recognize 3D shapes made by rotating 2D shapes.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GMD.B||Visualize r: Your child can picture how 2D and 3D shapes relate to each other.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GMD.A.3||Use volum: Your child can use formulas for the volume of cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GMD.A.2||(+) Give: Your child can explain informally why volume formulas for spheres and other solids work using Cavalieri's principle.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GMD.A.1||Give an i: Your child can explain informally the formulas for the circumference and area of circles, and volumes of cylinders, pyramids, and cones.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GMD.A||Explain vol: Your child can explain volume formulas and use them to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GPE.B.7||Use coord: Your child can use coordinates to find perimeters of polygons and areas of triangles and rectangles, like using the distance formula.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GPE.B.6||Find the: Your child can find a point on a line segment that divides it into a specific ratio.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GPE.B.5||Prove the: Your child can prove when lines are parallel or perpendicular using slopes and use this to solve geometry problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GPE.B.4||Use coord: Your child can use coordinates to prove simple geometry facts using algebra.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GPE.B||Use coordin: Your child can use coordinates to prove basic geometry theorems with algebra.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GPE.A.3||(+) Deriv: Your child can find equations for ellipses and hyperbolas using their special points called foci.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GPE.A.2||Derive th: Your child can find the equation of a parabola when given a focus point and a directrix line.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GPE.A.1||Derive th: Your child can write the equation of a circle using its center and radius and can find these from an equation.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-GPE.A||Translate b: Your child can change between the geometric description and the equation of conic sections like circles and parabolas.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-C.B.5||Derive usin: Your child can use similarity to show that the length of an arc is proportional to the radius and can find the area of a sector.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-C.B||Find arc leng: Your child can find the length of arcs and the area of sectors in circles.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-C.A.4||(+) Constru: Your child can draw a tangent line from a point outside a circle to touch the circle.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-C.A.3||Construct t: Your child can draw circles inside and around triangles and explain angle properties in quadrilaterals inside circles.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-C.A.2||Identify an: Your child can identify and explain how inscribed angles, radii, and chords relate in a circle.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-C.A.1||Prove that: Your child can prove that all circles are similar in shape.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-C.A||Understand an: Your child understands and uses important facts about circles.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.D.11||(+) Unde: Your child can use the Law of Sines and Cosines to find missing sides or angles in any triangle.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.D.10||(+) Prov: Your child can prove the Laws of Sines and Cosines and use them to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.D.9||(+) Deriv: Your child can find the area of a triangle using the formula 1/2 ab sin(C) by drawing a line inside the triangle.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.D||Apply trigo: Your child can use trigonometry to solve problems involving any kind of triangle.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.C.8||Use trigo: Your child can use trigonometric ratios and the Pythagorean Theorem to solve right triangle problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.C.7||Explain a: Your child can explain and use how sine and cosine of complementary angles relate to each other.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.C.6||Understan: Your child understands that in right triangles, the ratios of the sides depend on the angles, which helps define sine, cosine, and tangent for angles less than 90 degrees.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.C||Define trig: Your child can define sine, cosine, and tangent and use them to solve problems involving right triangles.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.B.5||Use congr: Your child uses rules about when triangles are the same shape or size to solve problems and prove facts about shapes.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.B.4||Prove the: Your child can prove important facts about triangles.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.B||Prove theor: Your child can prove facts about shapes that are similar.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.A.3||Use the p: Your child uses the idea of similar shapes to show that two triangles are similar if two pairs of angles are the same.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.A.2||Given two: Your child can decide if two shapes are similar by comparing their angles and sides, and explain this using transformations that resize and move shapes.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.A.1b||The dila: Your child understands that when a line segment is resized, its new length changes by the scale factor.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.A.1a||A dilati: Your child knows that resizing a line not through the center makes a parallel line, and resizing a line through the center keeps it the same.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.A.1||Verify ex: Your child can test and confirm how resizing shapes works using a center point and scale factor.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-SRT.A||Understand: Your child understands similarity by learning how shapes can be resized and moved to match each other.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.D.13||Construct: Your child can draw an equilateral triangle, a square, and a regular hexagon inside a circle.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.D.12||Make form: Your child can use tools like a compass and straightedge to copy segments and angles, split segments and angles in half, and draw perpendicular and parallel lines.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.C.11||Prove the: Your child can prove important facts about parallelograms.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.C.10||Prove the: Your child can prove important facts about triangles.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.C.9||Prove theo: Your child can prove important facts about lines and angles.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.B.8||Explain ho: Your child can explain why certain rules for triangle congruence (ASA, SAS, SSS) work by understanding how shapes can be moved without changing size or shape.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.B.7||Use the de: Your child can show that two triangles are exactly the same if their sides and angles match, using movements that don’t change size or shape.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.B.6||Use geomet: Your child can move shapes using flips, slides, and turns, predict what happens, and decide if two shapes are the same by these moves.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.B||Understand c: Your child understands that shapes are congruent if one can be moved to match the other without changing size or shape.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.A.5||Given a ge: Your child can draw a shape after it has been rotated, flipped, or slid, and can describe steps to move one shape onto another.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.A.4||Develop de: Your child learns what rotations, reflections, and translations are by using angles, circles, and lines.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.A.3||Given a re: Your child can describe how certain shapes like rectangles and regular polygons can be rotated or flipped to look the same.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.A.2||Represent: Your child can show and describe how shapes move in the plane using tools or software, and compare moves that keep size and shape to those that don’t.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.A.1||Know preci: Your child knows clear definitions for angle, circle, perpendicular and parallel lines, and line segments based on basic ideas like points and lines.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSG-CO.A||Experiment w: Your child explores how shapes change when they are moved, flipped, or resized on a flat surface.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-MD.B.7||(+) Analyz: Your child can use probability to analyze real-life decisions and strategies, like testing products or making game choices.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-MD.B.6||(+) Use pr: Your child can use probability to help make fair decisions, like drawing names or using random numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-MD.B.5b||Evaluate: Your child can compare different choices by calculating their expected results.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-MD.B.5a||Find the: Your child can find the average expected winnings or losses in a game of chance.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-MD.B.5||(+) Weigh: Your child can weigh different possible outcomes by assigning probabilities and calculating expected values.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-MD.B||Use probabil: Your child uses probability to understand and evaluate the results of different decisions.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-MD.A.4||(+) Develo: Your child can create a probability distribution from real data and find the expected value.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-MD.A.3||(+) Develo: Your child can create a probability distribution when probabilities can be calculated and find the expected value.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-MD.A.2||(+) Calcul: Your child can calculate the expected value of a random variable and understand it as the average outcome.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-MD.A.1||(+) Define: Your child can assign numbers to outcomes in a situation and graph the probability distribution like a data graph.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-MD.A||Calculate ex: Your child can calculate expected values and use them to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-CP.B.9||(+) Use pe: Your child can use counting methods to find probabilities of combined events and solve related problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-CP.B.8||(+) Apply: Your child can use the multiplication rule to find the probability of two events happening together and explain the result.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-CP.B.7||Apply the: Your child can use the addition rule to find the probability of one event or another happening and explain the result.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-CP.B.6||Find the c: Your child can find the chance of event A happening if event B has already happened by looking at how many outcomes of B also happen in A, and explain what this means.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-CP.B||Use the rule: Your child can use probability rules to figure out the chance of combined events happening when all outcomes are equally likely.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-CP.A.5||Recognize: Your child understands and can explain what conditional probability and independence mean using everyday examples.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-CP.A.4||Construct: Your child can make and read tables that show how two categories relate, and use these tables to decide if events are independent and to estimate conditional probabilities.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-CP.A.3||Understand: Your child understands that the chance of A happening if B happens is the chance of both happening divided by the chance of B, and knows that A and B are independent if knowing one happens doesn't change the chance of the other.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-CP.A.2||Understand: Your child knows that two events are independent if the chance of both happening together equals the chance of one times the chance of the other, and can use this to check independence.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-CP.A.1||Describe e: Your child can describe events by listing possible outcomes or by combining or excluding other events using 'or,' 'and,' and 'not.'
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-CP.A||Understand i: Your child understands what independence and conditional probability mean and can use these ideas to explain data.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-IC.B.6||Evaluate r: Your child can look at reports that use data and decide if the conclusions make sense.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-IC.B.5||Use data f: Your child can use data from experiments to compare two treatments and use simulations to see if differences are meaningful.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-IC.B.4||Use data f: Your child can use survey data to estimate averages or proportions for a whole group and figure out how much error might be in the estimate using simulations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-IC.B.3||Recognize: Your child knows the differences between surveys, experiments, and observational studies and understands how random choices are used in each.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-IC.B||Make inferen: Your child can make guesses and explain conclusions based on surveys, experiments, and observational studies.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-IC.A.2||Decide if: Your child can check if a given model matches results from data by using simulations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-IC.A.1||Understand: Your child understands that statistics is a way to learn about a whole group by studying a random sample from it.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-IC.A||Understand a: Your child understands and can evaluate random processes used in statistical experiments.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.C.9||Distinguis: Your child can tell the difference between when two things happen together and when one actually causes the other.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.C.8||Compute (u: Your child can use technology to calculate and explain the strength of a straight-line relationship between two variables.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.C.7||Interpret: Your child can explain what the slope and starting point of a line mean in the context of the data.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.B.6c||Fit a lin: Your child can draw a straight line that best fits a scatter plot when the points show a linear pattern.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.B.6b||Informall: Your child can check how well a function fits data by looking at the leftover differences after fitting.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.B.6a||Fit a fun: Your child can find a function that fits data and use it to solve problems related to that data.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.B.6||Represent: Your child can make a scatter plot with two number variables and describe how they relate.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.B.5||Summarize: Your child can summarize data with two categories using tables and explain the frequencies and possible patterns in the data.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.B||Summarize, r: Your child can summarize, show, and explain data that includes two categories or numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.A.4||Use the me: Your child can use the average and spread of data to fit it to a normal curve and estimate percentages, knowing when this method works and when it doesn't.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.A.3||Interpret: Your child can explain differences in the shape, center, and spread of data sets, including how outliers affect them.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.A.2||Use statis: Your child can use the right statistics to compare the center and spread of different data sets based on their shape.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.A.1||Represent: Your child can show data using dot plots, histograms, and box plots on a number line.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSS-ID.A||Summarize, r: Your child can summarize, show, and explain data that counts or measures one thing.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.C.12||(+) Work: Your child can work with 2 by 2 matrices as ways to change the plane and understand that the size of the determinant shows how area changes.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.C.11||(+) Multi: Your child can multiply a vector by a matrix to get another vector and use matrices to transform vectors.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.C.10||(+) Under: Your child understands that zero and identity matrices act like 0 and 1 in addition and multiplication, and that a matrix has an inverse only if its determinant isn't zero.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.C.9||(+) Unders: Your child knows that matrix multiplication is not like regular number multiplication because order matters, but it still follows associative and distributive rules.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.C.8||(+) Add, s: Your child can add, subtract, and multiply matrices when their sizes match.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.C.7||(+) Multip: Your child can multiply matrices by numbers to create new matrices, like doubling all values in a game payoff.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.C.6||(+) Use ma: Your child can use matrices to organize and work with data, such as showing payoffs or connections in a network.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.C||Perform oper: Your child can perform operations on matrices and use them to solve real problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.B.5b||Compute t: Your child can find the length of a vector multiplied by a number and know that the direction depends on whether the number is positive or negative.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.B.5a||Represent: Your child can show multiplying a vector by a number by stretching or flipping it and can multiply each part of the vector by that number.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.B.5||(+) Multip: Your child can multiply a vector by a number to make it longer or shorter.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.B.4c||Understan: Your child understands how to subtract one vector from another by flipping the second vector and adding it, and can show this on a graph and by subtracting parts.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.B.4b||Given two: Your child can find the length and direction of the result when adding two vectors given by their lengths and directions.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.B.4a||Add vecto: Your child can add vectors by placing them end to end, adding their parts, or using the parallelogram method, and knows the total length is usually not just the sum of the lengths.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.B||Perform oper: Your child can do math operations like adding, subtracting, and multiplying with vectors.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.A.3||(+) Solve: Your child can solve problems involving speed and direction using vectors.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.A.2||(+) Find t: Your child can find the parts of a vector by subtracting the starting point coordinates from the ending point coordinates.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.A.1||(+) Recogn: Your child understands that vectors have both length and direction, and can draw them as arrows with correct labels.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-VM.A||Represent an: Your child can represent and work with quantities that have both size and direction using vectors.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.C.9||(+) Know t: Your child knows the Fundamental Theorem of Algebra and can show it works for quadratic equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.C.8||(+) Extend: Your child can use polynomial rules with complex numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.C.7||Solve quad: Your child can solve quadratic equations that have complex (non-real) answers.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.C||Use complex: Your child can use complex numbers when working with polynomial equations and identities.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.B.6||(+) Calcul: Your child can find the distance between complex numbers and the midpoint between them on the complex plane.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.B.5||(+) Repres: Your child can show adding, subtracting, multiplying, and conjugating complex numbers on a graph and use this to help with calculations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.B.4||(+) Repres: Your child can represent complex numbers on a graph in both rectangular (x and y) and polar (angle and length) forms and explain why these are the same number.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.B||Represent co: Your child can show complex numbers and their operations on a graph.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.A.3||(+) Find t: Your child can find the conjugate of a complex number and use it to calculate lengths and divide complex numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.A.2||Use the re: Your child can add, subtract, and multiply complex numbers using the rule that i squared equals -1 and properties of operations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.A.1||Know there: Your child knows that the number i satisfies i² = -1 and that every complex number can be written as a + bi where a and b are real numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-CN.A||Perform arit: Your child can do addition, subtraction, and multiplication with complex numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-Q.A.3||Choose a le: Your child can choose how exact to be when measuring and reporting quantities based on how precise the measurements are.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-Q.A.2||Define appr: Your child can decide which quantities to use when creating models to describe real situations.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-Q.A.1||Use units a: Your child uses units to understand problems, solve multi-step problems, and correctly choose units and scales on graphs.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-Q.A||Reason quanti: Your child can think about quantities and use units to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-RN.B.3||Explain wh: Your child can explain why adding or multiplying two rational numbers gives a rational number, and why adding or multiplying with irrational numbers usually gives an irrational number.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-RN.B||Use properti: Your child understands the rules for working with rational and irrational numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-RN.A.2||Rewrite ex: Your child can rewrite expressions with roots and fractional exponents using exponent rules.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-RN.A.1||Explain ho: Your child understands that fractional exponents come from extending the rules of whole number exponents and can use them to write roots.
CCSS.Math.Content.HSN-RN.A||Extend the p: Your child can use exponent rules with fractional exponents.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.G.A.3||Partition cir: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into halves and quarters, use words like halves and fourths to describe them, and understand that more pieces mean smaller parts.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.G.A.2||Compose two-d: Your child can put together flat shapes or solid shapes to make new shapes and then create even more shapes from those.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.G.A.1||Distinguish b: Your child can tell the difference between important shape features like having three sides for triangles and less important features like color or size, and can make shapes with the important features.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.G.A||Reason with sha: Your child can think about shapes and their important features.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.MD.C.4||Organize, re: Your child can organize and understand data with up to three groups, and ask and answer questions about how many items are in each group and compare the groups.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.MD.B.3||Tell and wri: Your child can tell and write time to the hour and half-hour using both analog and digital clocks.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.MD.A.2||Express the: Your child can measure how long something is by lining up smaller objects end to end without gaps or overlaps and counting how many fit.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.MD.A.1||Order three: Your child can put three objects in order from shortest to longest and compare lengths by using a third object.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.MD.A||Measure length: Your child can measure lengths by using repeated units and by comparing lengths indirectly.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.NBT.C.6||Subtract mu: Your child can subtract multiples of 10 from each other using drawings or strategies based on place value, explain their method, and connect it to written subtraction.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.NBT.C.5||Given a two: Your child can quickly find 10 more or 10 less than any two-digit number in their head and explain how they figured it out.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.NBT.C.4||Add within: Your child can add numbers up to 100, including adding a two-digit number with a one-digit number or a multiple of 10, using drawings or objects and explaining their thinking.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.NBT.C||Use place val: Your child understands how to use place value and math rules to add and subtract numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.NBT.B.3||Compare two: Your child can compare two two-digit numbers by looking at their tens and ones digits and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.NBT.B.2c||The number: Your child understands that numbers like 10, 20, 30, and so on mean one, two, three, etc. groups of ten and zero ones.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.NBT.B.2b||The number: Your child knows that numbers from 11 to 19 are made of one ten and some extra ones.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.NBT.B.2a||10 can be: Your child understands that 10 is a group of ten ones, called a 'ten.'
CCSS.Math.Content.1.NBT.B.2||Understand: Your child understands that the two digits in a two-digit number show how many tens and ones there are.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.NBT.A.1||Count to 12: Your child can count to 120 starting from any number below 120, read and write numbers in this range, and match numbers to groups of objects.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.OA.D.8||Determine th: Your child can find the missing number in addition or subtraction problems with three whole numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.OA.D.7||Understand t: Your child understands what the equal sign means and can tell if addition or subtraction equations are true or false.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.OA.D||Work with addi: Your child works with addition and subtraction equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.OA.C.6||Add and subt: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 20 using strategies like counting on, making ten, breaking numbers apart, and using what they know about addition and subtraction.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.OA.C.5||Relate count: Your child connects counting with adding and subtracting, like counting on to add.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.OA.B.4||Understand s: Your child understands subtraction as finding the missing addend in an addition problem.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.OA.B.3||Apply proper: Your child uses math rules to help add and subtract.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.OA.B||Understand and: Your child understands and uses math rules and the connection between addition and subtraction.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.OA.A.2||Solve word p: Your child can solve word problems that add three whole numbers with a total of 20 or less, using objects, drawings, or equations with a missing number.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.OA.A.1||Use addition: Your child solves word problems with addition and subtraction within 20, using objects, drawings, or equations with a missing number.
CCSS.Math.Content.1.OA.A||Represent and: Your child can show and solve problems using addition and subtraction.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.C.8c||Design and: Your child can create and use simulations to see how often combined events happen.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.C.8b||Represent s: Your child can list all possible outcomes for combined events using lists, tables, or tree diagrams and identify which outcomes match an event described in everyday words.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.C.8a||Understand: Your child understands that the chance of combined events happening is the part of all possible outcomes where those events occur.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.C.8||Find probabi: Your child can find the chances of combined events using lists, tables, tree diagrams, and simulations.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.C.7b||Develop a p: Your child can create a probability model by watching how often things happen in a chance process, even if outcomes don’t all have the same chance.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.C.7a||Develop a u: Your child can make a probability model where all outcomes are equally likely and use it to find chances of events.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.C.7||Develop a pr: Your child can create a probability model, use it to find chances of events, and compare those chances to what actually happens, explaining differences.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.C.6||Approximate: Your child can estimate the chance of an event by collecting data and watching how often it happens over time, and predict how often it should happen.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.C.5||Understand t: Your child understands that probability is a number from 0 to 1 showing how likely an event is, with numbers near 0 meaning unlikely, near 1/2 meaning about even chance, and near 1 meaning very likely.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.C||Investigate ch: Your child explores chance events and creates, uses, and checks probability models.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.B.4||Use measures: Your child uses averages and spread of data from random samples to make simple comparisons between two groups.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.B.3||Informally a: Your child looks at how two sets of data overlap and measures the difference between their centers compared to how spread out the data is.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.B||Draw informal: Your child makes simple comparisons between two groups of data.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.A.2||Use data fro: Your child uses data from random samples to make guesses about a larger group and takes several samples to see how much the guesses can change.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.A.1||Understand t: Your child understands that looking at a sample can tell us about a whole group only if the sample fairly represents the group, and that random samples usually do this.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.SP.A||Use random sam: Your child uses random sampling to learn about a larger group.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.EE.B.4b||Solve word: Your child can solve word problems that lead to inequalities like px + q > r or px + q < r, graph the answers, and explain what the graph means for the problem.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.EE.B.4a||Solve word: Your child can solve word problems that lead to equations like px + q = r or p(x + q) = r, solve these equations easily, and compare algebra and arithmetic methods.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.EE.B.4||Use variable: Your child uses letters to stand for numbers in real-life or math problems and writes simple equations or inequalities to solve them by thinking about the numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.EE.B.3||Solve multi-: Your child solves multi-step real-life and math problems with positive and negative numbers in different forms, uses tools wisely, changes forms when needed, and checks if answers make sense.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.EE.B||Solve real-lif: Your child can solve everyday and math problems using numbers and simple algebra expressions and equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.EE.A.2||Understand t: Your child understands that changing how an expression looks can help explain the problem and how the amounts relate.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.EE.A.1||Apply proper: Your child can use math rules to add, subtract, factor, and expand simple algebra expressions with fractions or decimals.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.EE.A||Use properties: Your child uses math rules to create different but equal expressions.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A.3||Solve real-w: Your child can solve real-life and math problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with fractions and decimals.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A.2d||Convert a r: Your child can change a fraction or ratio into a decimal by dividing and knows decimals either end or repeat.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A.2c||Apply prope: Your child uses math rules to multiply and divide fractions and decimals.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A.2b||Understand: Your child understands dividing whole numbers works as long as you don’t divide by zero, and the answer is a fraction or decimal; they can explain what division means in real life.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A.2a||Understand: Your child knows how to multiply positive and negative numbers using math rules and can explain what multiplying fractions and decimals means in real life.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A.2||Apply and ex: Your child can multiply and divide fractions and decimals, building on what they already know about these operations.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A.1d||Apply prope: Your child uses math rules to add and subtract fractions and decimals.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A.1c||Understand: Your child understands subtracting fractions and decimals means adding the opposite number and can find the distance between two numbers on a number line.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A.1b||Understand: Your child knows adding a positive or negative number moves along the number line and that a number plus its opposite equals zero; they can explain this with real examples.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A.1a||Describe si: Your child can describe situations where two opposite amounts add up to zero.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A.1||Apply and ex: Your child can add and subtract fractions and decimals and show these on a number line going left-right or up-down.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.NS.A||Apply and exte: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions and decimals using what they already know about fractions.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.RP.A.3||Use proporti: Your child can solve multi-step problems about ratios and percentages using proportional relationships.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.RP.A.2d||Explain wha: Your child can explain what points on a graph mean in a proportional relationship, especially the points (0,0) and (1, unit rate).
CCSS.Math.Content.7.RP.A.2c||Represent p: Your child can write equations that show proportional relationships.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.RP.A.2b||Identify th: Your child can find the unit rate (constant ratio) in tables, graphs, equations, pictures, or words about proportional relationships.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.RP.A.2a||Decide whet: Your child can tell if two amounts are proportional by checking if their ratios are equal or if their graph is a straight line through zero.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.RP.A.2||Recognize an: Your child can recognize and show proportional relationships between amounts.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.RP.A.1||Compute unit: Your child can find unit rates (like speed or price per item) when given ratios with fractions, including lengths and areas.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.RP.A||Analyze propor: Your child can study proportional relationships and use them to solve real-life and math problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.G.B.6||Solve real-wo: Your child can solve problems about area, volume, and surface area of shapes made from triangles, squares, polygons, cubes, and right prisms.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.G.B.5||Use facts abo: Your child can use facts about different types of angles to write and solve simple equations to find unknown angles in shapes.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.G.B.4||Know the form: Your child knows the formulas for the area and circumference of a circle and can use them to solve problems and explain how circumference and area relate.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.G.B||Solve real-life: Your child can solve real-life and math problems involving angles, area, surface area, and volume.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.G.A.3||Describe the: Your child can describe the flat shapes you get when slicing 3D shapes like rectangular prisms and pyramids.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.G.A.2||Draw (freehan: Your child can draw shapes by hand, with tools, or on a computer using given measurements, and can tell when those measurements make one, more than one, or no triangle.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.G.A.1||Solve problem: Your child can solve problems using scale drawings, including finding real lengths and areas and making new scale drawings.
CCSS.Math.Content.7.G.A||Draw, construct: Your child can draw, build, and describe shapes and explain how they relate to each other.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.7c||Solve real: Your child can solve real problems dividing unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions using pictures or equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.7b||Interpret d: Your child can understand and calculate what happens when you divide a whole number by a unit fraction.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.7a||Interpret d: Your child can understand and calculate what happens when you divide a unit fraction by a whole number.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.7||Apply and ex: Your child can divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions, building on what they already know about division.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.6||Solve real w: Your child can solve real problems multiplying fractions and mixed numbers using pictures or equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.5b||Explaining: Your child can explain why multiplying by a fraction bigger than 1 makes a number bigger, and multiplying by a fraction less than 1 makes it smaller, and understand multiplying fractions by 1 keeps them the same.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.5a||Comparing t: Your child can compare the size of a multiplication result to one factor by looking at the other factor without actually multiplying.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.5||Interpret mu: Your child understands multiplication as resizing or scaling.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.4b||Find the ar: Your child can find the area of rectangles with fractional side lengths by counting small squares and by multiplying the side lengths.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.4a||Interpret t: Your child understands multiplying a fraction by a whole number as taking parts of that whole number or by multiplying and dividing in steps.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.4||Apply and ex: Your child can multiply a fraction by another fraction or by a whole number.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B.3||Interpret a: Your child understands a fraction as dividing the top number by the bottom number and can solve word problems using this idea.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.B||Apply and exte: Your child can multiply and divide fractions and whole numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.A.2||Solve word p: Your child can solve word problems adding and subtracting fractions with different denominators using pictures or equations and can estimate if answers make sense.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.A.1||Add and subt: Your child can add and subtract fractions with different denominators by changing them to have the same denominator first.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NF.A||Use equivalent: Your child uses equivalent fractions to help add and subtract fractions.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.G.B.4||Classify two-: Your child can sort flat shapes into groups based on their properties, like sides and angles.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.G.B.3||Understand th: Your child understands that if a shape belongs to a big group, it also has the properties of smaller groups inside it.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.G.B||Classify two-di: Your child can put flat shapes into categories by looking at their features.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.G.A.2||Represent rea: Your child can plot points on a graph in the first quadrant and explain what the points mean in real situations.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.G.A.1||Use a pair of: Your child understands how to use two number lines that cross at zero to find points using pairs of numbers called coordinates.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.G.A||Graph points on: Your child can graph points on a coordinate plane to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.C.5c||Recognize v: Your child can find the volume of solid shapes made by joining two rectangular boxes by adding their volumes.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.C.5b||Apply the f: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of rectangular boxes with whole number sides in real problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.C.5a||Find the vo: Your child can find the volume of a rectangular box by filling it with unit cubes and by multiplying its length, width, and height.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.C.5||Relate volum: Your child understands volume relates to multiplication and addition and can solve problems about volume.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.C.4||Measure volu: Your child can measure volume by counting unit cubes using different units like cubic centimeters or cubic inches.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.C.3b||A solid fig: Your child knows that a solid made by fitting together n unit cubes has a volume of n cubic units.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.C.3a||A cube with: Your child understands a cube with sides one unit long is called a unit cube and has a volume of one cubic unit.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.C.3||Recognize vo: Your child recognizes volume as a property of solid shapes and understands how to measure it.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.C||Geometric meas: Your child understands volume and how it connects to multiplication and addition.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.B.2||Make a line: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements with fractions and use fraction math to solve problems from the plot.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.A.1||Convert amon: Your child can change measurements from one unit to another within the same system and use these changes to solve multi-step problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.MD.A||Convert like m: Your child can convert between different units of measurement within the same system.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NBT.B.7||Add, subtra: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths using models and strategies, and explain how they solved the problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NBT.B.6||Find whole-: Your child can divide whole numbers with up to four digits by two-digit numbers using place value and other strategies and explain their work.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NBT.B.5||Fluently mu: Your child can multiply multi-digit whole numbers quickly and accurately using the standard method.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NBT.B||Perform opera: Your child can do addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with multi-digit whole numbers and decimals to hundredths.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NBT.A.4||Use place v: Your child can round decimals to any place value using their understanding of place value.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NBT.A.3b||Compare tw: Your child can compare two decimals to thousandths and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NBT.A.3a||Read and w: Your child can read and write decimals to thousandths in numbers, words, and expanded form.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NBT.A.3||Read, write: Your child can read, write, and compare decimals up to thousandths.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NBT.A.2||Explain pat: Your child understands how multiplying or dividing by powers of 10 changes the number and decimal point, and can explain the patterns.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NBT.A.1||Recognize t: Your child knows that each digit in a number is ten times bigger than the digit to its right and one tenth the size of the digit to its left.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.NBT.A||Understand th: Your child understands how the place value system works.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.OA.B.3||Generate two: Your child can create two number patterns from rules, find relationships between them, and graph pairs of matching numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.OA.B||Analyze patter: Your child can look at patterns and relationships between numbers.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.OA.A.2||Write simple: Your child can write simple math expressions to show calculations and understand what the expressions mean without solving them.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.OA.A.1||Use parenthe: Your child can use parentheses, brackets, or braces in math expressions and solve problems that include these symbols.
CCSS.Math.Content.5.OA.A||Write and inte: Your child can write math expressions with numbers and explain what they mean.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.G.B.6||Compose simpl: Your child can put simple shapes together to make bigger shapes.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.G.B.5||Model shapes: Your child can build shapes using objects like sticks and clay and draw shapes they see around them.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.G.B.4||Analyze and c: Your child can look at flat and solid shapes of different sizes and describe how they are alike and different, including parts like sides and corners.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.G.B||Analyze, compar: Your child can look at, compare, create, and put together different shapes.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.G.A.3||Identify shap: Your child understands the difference between flat shapes (2D) and solid shapes (3D).
CCSS.Math.Content.K.G.A.2||Correctly nam: Your child can name shapes correctly no matter how they are turned or how big they are.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.G.A.1||Describe obje: Your child can name shapes they see and describe where things are using words like above, below, beside, in front of, behind, and next to.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.G.A||Identify and de: Your child can recognize and talk about shapes like squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.MD.B.3||Classify obj: Your child can sort objects into groups, count how many are in each group, and put the groups in order by size.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.MD.B||Classify objec: Your child can sort objects into categories and count how many are in each category.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.MD.A.2||Directly com: Your child can compare two objects to see which one has more or less of something like length or weight and explain the difference.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.MD.A.1||Describe mea: Your child can describe things about objects that can be measured, like how long or heavy they are, and talk about several of these features for one object.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.MD.A||Describe and c: Your child can describe and compare things about objects that can be measured.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.NBT.A.1||Compose and: Your child can break apart and put together numbers from 11 to 19 using groups of ten and extra ones, and show this with drawings or equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.NBT.A||Work with num: Your child works with numbers 11 to 19 to understand how place value works.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.OA.A.5||Fluently add: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 5.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.OA.A.4||For any numb: Your child can find the number that adds to another number from 1 to 9 to make 10, using objects or drawings, and write it down.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.OA.A.3||Decompose nu: Your child can break numbers up to 10 into pairs in different ways and show this with drawings or equations.
