CCSS.Math.Content.K.OA.A.2||Solve additi: Your child can solve simple addition and subtraction story problems using objects or drawings.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.OA.A.1||Represent ad: Your child can show addition and subtraction using objects, fingers, pictures, sounds, acting, talking, or writing.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.OA.A||Understand add: Your child understands that addition means putting things together and subtraction means taking things apart or away.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.C.7||Compare two: Your child can compare two numbers from 1 to 10 written as numerals to see which is bigger or smaller.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.C.6||Identify whe: Your child can tell if one group has more, less, or the same number of objects as another group by matching or counting.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.B.5||Count to ans: Your child can count up to 20 things arranged in a line, rectangle, or circle, or up to 10 things scattered, and can count out a given number of objects up to 20.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.B.4c||Understand: Your child understands that each number name you say when counting means one more than the number before.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.B.4b||Understand: Your child knows that the last number said when counting tells how many objects there are, no matter how they are arranged or counted.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.B.4a||When counti: Your child counts objects by saying number names in order, matching one number to each object and one object to each number.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.B.4||Understand t: Your child understands how numbers and amounts are connected and that counting tells how many things there are.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.B||Count to tell: Your child can count to find out how many objects there are.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.A.3||Write number: Your child can write numbers from 0 to 20 and use these numbers to show how many objects there are, including zero for none.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.A.2||Count forwar: Your child can count forward starting from any number they know, not just from 1.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.A.1||Count to 100: Your child can count to 100 by ones and by tens.
CCSS.Math.Content.K.CC.A||Know number na: Your child knows number names and the order of counting.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.F.B.5||Describe qual: Your child can look at a graph and describe how two things are related, like when one goes up or down, and can draw a graph based on a description.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.F.B.4||Construct a f: Your child can create a math rule (function) to show a straight-line relationship between two things, find how fast one changes compared to the other, and explain what these numbers mean using tables or graphs.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.F.B||Use functions t: Your child can use functions to show how two quantities are connected.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.F.A.3||Interpret the: Your child understands that the equation y = mx + b makes a straight line on a graph and can give examples of functions that don’t make straight lines.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.F.A.2||Compare prope: Your child can compare two functions shown in different ways, like with equations, graphs, tables, or words.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.F.A.1||Understand th: Your child understands that a function is a rule that gives exactly one output for each input, and can see this as pairs of inputs and outputs on a graph.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.F.A||Define, evaluat: Your child can define what a function is, find its value for given inputs, and compare different functions.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.SP.A.4||Understand t: Your child can make and read two-way tables that show how two categories relate, using counts and percentages to describe their connection.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.SP.A.3||Use the equa: Your child can use a straight-line equation to solve problems with two related measurements and explain what the slope and starting point mean.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.SP.A.2||Know that st: Your child knows that straight lines often show relationships between two numbers and can draw a line that fits data points to see how well it matches.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.SP.A.1||Construct an: Your child can make and understand scatter plots to find patterns between two quantities, like groups, unusual points, or types of relationships.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.SP.A||Investigate pa: Your child explores how two sets of data relate to each other.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.C.8c||Solve real-: Your child can solve real-life and math problems that involve two linear equations with two variables.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.C.8b||Solve syste: Your child can solve pairs of linear equations using algebra, estimate answers by graphing, and solve simple cases by looking.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.C.8a||Understand: Your child understands that the solution to two linear equations is where their graphs cross, because that point fits both equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.C.8||Analyze and: Your child can analyze and solve pairs of linear equations that happen at the same time.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.C.7b||Solve linea: Your child can solve linear equations with fractions and decimals, including those needing to expand expressions and combine like terms.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.C.7a||Give exampl: Your child can give examples of one-variable linear equations that have one solution, many solutions, or no solution, and show which by simplifying the equation.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.C.7||Solve linear: Your child can solve linear equations with one variable.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.C||Analyze and so: Your child can analyze and solve both single linear equations and pairs of linear equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.B.6||Use similar: Your child can explain why the slope is the same between any two points on a line and can write the equation of a line through the origin or with a vertical intercept.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.B.5||Graph propor: Your child can graph proportional relationships, understand the unit rate as the slope, and compare different proportional relationships shown in various ways.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.B||Understand the: Your child understands how proportional relationships connect to lines and linear equations.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.A.4||Perform oper: Your child can do math with numbers in scientific notation, choose suitable units for very big or small measurements, and understand scientific notation from technology.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.A.3||Use numbers: Your child can use numbers written as a digit times a power of 10 to estimate very large or small amounts and compare how many times bigger one is than another.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.A.2||Use square r: Your child can use square root and cube root symbols to solve equations like x² = p or x³ = p, find roots of small perfect squares and cubes, and knows that √2 cannot be written as a simple fraction.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.A.1||Know and app: Your child knows and uses the rules for integer exponents to rewrite numerical expressions in different but equal ways.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.EE.A||Work with radi: Your child works with roots and integer exponents.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.NS.A.2||Use rational: Your child can use close rational numbers to compare and estimate irrational numbers and place them roughly on a number line.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.NS.A.1||Know that nu: Your child knows that irrational numbers are not fractions, understands that all numbers have decimal forms, and can turn repeating decimals into fractions.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.NS.A||Know that ther: Your child knows some numbers are not fractions and can approximate them using fractions.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.G.C.9||Know the form: Your child knows the formulas for the volumes of cones, cylinders, and spheres and can use them to solve problems.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.G.C||Solve real-worl: Your child can solve real-life and math problems involving the volume of cylinders, cones, and spheres.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.G.B.8||Apply the Pyt: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between two points on a coordinate grid.
CCSS.Math.Content.8.G.B.7||Apply the Pyt: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find missing side lengths in right triangles in both two- and three-dimensional problems.

CCSS.ELA-Literacy.L.11-12.6||Acquire and: Your child can learn and use academic and subject-specific words correctly when reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

CCSS.ELA-Literacy.SL.11-12.6||Adapt spee: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

CCSS.ELA-Literacy.RST.11-12.10||By the e: Your child can read and understand challenging science and technical texts on their own by the end of 12th grade.
